meta.name: Program Manager MCP

swimlanes.label: Phase 0: Design Contract
swimlanes.description: Architecture, contracts, threat model, and fixtures that must be stable before implementation.

swimlanes.label: Phase 1A: Read-Only Graph, Seed, and Impact MVP
swimlanes.description: First useful PMO MVP: graph-backed memory, impact analysis, read-only adapters, authz, redaction, and deterministic fixture proof.

swimlanes.label: Phase 1B: Context Packets, Reports, Adapter Health
swimlanes.description: Bounded context panes, reproducible reports, adapter degradation behavior, audit trail, and PMO operational checks.

swimlanes.label: Phase 1C: Learning and Decision Intelligence
swimlanes.description: Evidence-backed learnings, attempts, discarded decisions, failure patterns, risk signals, and deterministic intelligence cards.

swimlanes.label: Phase 2: Flight Plans and Expected Receipts
swimlanes.description: Deterministic flight plans, approval/evidence obligations, proposal-only downstream actions, expected receipts, TTL, and loop suppression.

swimlanes.label: Phase 3: Receipt Ledger and Reconciliation
swimlanes.description: Receipt recording, expected-vs-observed ledger, audit persistence, desynchronization findings, and compensating plan proposals.

swimlanes.label: Phase 4: Agentic OS Integration and Operational Hardening
swimlanes.description: Agentic OS consumption, receipt submission integration, trust root, tenant hardening, retention/PII policy, OTel, replay, and operational smoke.

swimlanes.label: Phase 5: Macro Omni-Tool Surface
swimlanes.description: Single pmo_macro public tool, normalized PMO object model, editable safe macro registry, and deterministic macro implementations for blockers, context, impact simulation, drift, and acceptance proof.

swimlanes.label: Agent PMO Onboarding
swimlanes.description: Make real cross-project PMO setup discoverable and executable by agents without human-driven coordination.

swimlanes.label: PMO Omni-Tools
swimlanes.description: Close the agent-handoff gaps with domain omni-tools that own full lifecycle actions instead of narrow one-off PMO endpoints.

swimlanes.label: Cypher Rule Detectors
swimlanes.description: Versioned read-only Cypher detector rules, controlled Finding writes, report grouping, health scoring, APIs, and guardrails.

meta.scratchpad: Added PMO profile-variation tracker tasks. Current slice is documenting summary/operator/executor/auditor MCP registration in README and AGENTS.

tasks.title: ADR, data model, event replay, and hash spec
tasks.goal: Document Program Manager as a stateful PMO memory service with its own database, deterministic event/replay model, and canonical hashing rules before implementation begins.
expected_changes: Architecture ADR covering PMO boundaries, TypeScript, Neo4j, repository abstraction, and passive-analyst posture.
Data model spec for DependencyEdge, ArtifactRef, EvidencePolicy, ContextAnchor, events, replay, and hashes.
Parseable examples for stateVersionHash and flightPlanHash inputs.
definition_of_done: ADR states PMO owns its repository/artifact/event memory but does not mutate downstream tools.
Event/replay policy is explicit enough to rebuild graph projections deterministically.
Hash fixture computes the same digest from repeated canonical inputs.
Open Phase 0 questions are listed with owner/decision authority where unresolved.
constraints: Preserve pointer-only context and no downstream mutation boundary.
Do not defer authz, redaction, or tenant isolation assumptions out of the design contract.

context.notes: Verified the two JSON fixtures by recomputing canonical SHA-256 over the input objects and matching the stored expectedHash values. tracker_verify was run before completion.

tasks.comment: Completed the Phase 0 ADR plus the data model, event replay, and canonical hash spec with parseable stateVersionHash and flightPlanHash fixtures. The docs keep PMO pointer-only, passive, and non-mutating toward downstream systems, and they make authz, redaction, and portfolio isolation explicit design assumptions.

tasks.title: Schema examples and parseable fixture contracts
tasks.goal: Turn core PMO concepts into parseable schema examples and fixture documents that later implementation and conformance tests can reuse.
expected_changes: Examples for Portfolio, Program, Project, ContextAnchor, DependencyRelationshipProps, ArtifactRef, EvidenceRef, EvidencePolicy, DecisionRecord, and SyncCursor.
Fixture examples for G0/C0/A0/F0/H0 inputs and expected outputs.
Validation notes for generated JSON Schema and AJV-compatible examples.
definition_of_done: All schema/data examples parse under the chosen Zod/JSON Schema contract.
Fixture names and meanings match the golden fixture backbone.
Examples avoid raw logs, transcripts, screenshots, secrets, and content-bearing evidence inline.
The schema examples are ready to be copied into implementation tests without reinterpretation.
constraints: Keep examples pointer-only and deterministic.
Use stable IDs and explicit sorted ordering where fixture output depends on collection order.

context.notes: Validation: all Phase 0 JSON fixtures parsed with Node; structural checks confirmed required schema examples and G0/C0/A0/F0/H0 keys; stateVersionHash and flightPlanHash fixtures recomputed successfully.

tasks.comment: Completed schema examples, validation notes, and the shared G0/C0/A0/F0/H0 fixture backbone. The JSON examples parse, include the required core PMO concepts, and stay pointer-only with deterministic ordering rules documented.

tasks.title: Adapter, authz, approval, and security contracts
tasks.goal: Specify how PMO reads standalone capabilities through adapters and how identity, approval authority, isolation, redaction, and threat controls are enforced.
expected_changes: Adapter manifest spec covering capability domains, side-effect posture, health, freshness, cursor, redaction, and evidence types.
Impact assessment request/result schema with fixture examples for LLM Tracker and Hoplon.
Approval authority matrix for tier 0/1 gates, expiration, supersession, evidence, and break-glass handling.
Security threat model covering artifact URI abuse, SSRF, path traversal, poisoned adapter data, prompt injection, receipt forgery, and confused-deputy risks.
definition_of_done: Adapter conformance suite requirements are defined before adapter code exists.
Portfolio-scoped identity and trust-root requirements are explicit.
Every approval gate has required authority, evidence, expiration/review behavior, and supersession semantics.
Threat model identifies concrete mitigations or tests for each listed risk.
constraints: Adapters are read-only in Phase 1A and must not hide mutation-capable downstream actions.
Do not rely on an LLM to remember propagation, redaction, or approval rules.

context.notes: Validation: adapter fixture JSON parsed; structural checks confirmed explicit side-effect posture and required approval authority/evidence fields. tracker_verify was run before completion.

tasks.comment: Completed adapter, authz, approval, and security contracts with read-only adapter posture, trust-root and portfolio isolation assumptions, approval gate semantics, conformance suite requirements, and threat model tests.

tasks.title: Public PMO tool contracts and result envelope
tasks.goal: Define the small MCP surface and a standard result envelope so every PMO tool returns deterministic core data, evidence refs, artifacts, warnings, and redaction metadata consistently.
expected_changes: Tool contracts for list_program_capabilities, get_program_documentation, query_program_context, and assess_program_impact.
Forward-compatible contracts for generate_program_update, get_program_audit_trail, analyze_program_intelligence, plan_program_action, record_program_receipt, and reconcile_program_state.
ProgramToolResultEnvelope schema with deterministicCore/advisoryPane separation, evidenceRefs, artifactRefs, warnings, trace, correlation, and redaction summary.
definition_of_done: Phase 1A tools are fully specified with request/response examples and authz/redaction expectations.
Later tools are scoped enough that Phase 2/3 tasks can depend on them without guessing result shape.
Advisory/model-assisted content is explicitly excluded from deterministic hashes.
Tool envelope examples parse and include no raw sensitive/content-bearing payloads.
constraints: Keep the MCP surface macro-level; do not expose every downstream tool through PMO.
Every result must preserve the passive analyst boundary and carry evidence/provenance context.
tasks.comment: Completed public PMO tool contracts and result envelope. Added Phase 1A request/core examples, forward-compatible later tool scopes, authz/redaction expectations, and parseable envelope fixture examples.

tasks.title: Sample registry and golden fixture scope
tasks.goal: Define the sample program registry and fixture universe for Hoplon, Phalanx, Semantix, Guardrail, and Program Manager MCP so all later tests share one backbone.
expected_changes: Sample registry manifest for programs, projects, verticals, integration points, contracts, policies, evidence refs, decisions, and sync cursors.
Fixture scope for G0, C0, A0, F0, H0, P0, R0, and I0.
Known open questions for actual project IDs, tracker slugs, adapter sources, trust root, artifact store, URI allowlist, Neo4j features, retention, and approval authorities.
definition_of_done: Sample registry can seed a fixture graph without invented downstream mutation authority.
Fixture expected refs include at least one cross-project dependency and one stale/missing evidence condition.
Open questions from the blueprint are preserved as explicit follow-up decisions.
All later phase tasks can reference this fixture backbone instead of creating isolated examples.
constraints: Do not make LLM Tracker the durable PMO memory; registry is only an operational input/source reference.
Use allowlisted URI schemes as identifiers first, not arbitrary fetch instructions.
tasks.comment: Updated fixture wording to remove the prohibited legacy consumer references; fixture universe is Hoplon, Phalanx, Semantix, Guardrail, and Program Manager MCP.

InMemoryProgramManagerRepository skeleton for narrow unit tests.

Repository interface covers Phase 1A read paths without leaking Neo4j/Cypher into services.
In-memory repository supports unit fixture setup for schema and service tests.
Boundary checks confirm the package does not import product app/server code.
constraints: Do not introduce downstream mutation APIs in the PMO package.
Keep implementation consistent with existing control-plane package patterns.
tasks.comment: Completed Phase 1A package skeleton/repository boundary. Added cp-program-manager scaffold, repository interface, in-memory fixture repository, package exports, and boundary tests. Verified npm test, schema generation, and tsc --noEmit via local Hoplon toolchain.

tasks.comment: Completed shared Zod schema layer and deterministic JSON Schema export. Phase 0 examples, golden fixture, tool contracts, and adapter fixtures parse; generated schema snapshots are stable and pointer-only envelope checks pass.

tasks.title: Golden fixture backbone

expected_changes: Fixture data for G0 seed graph, C0 proposed change, A0 affected refs, F0 findings, and H0 stateVersionHash.
Fixture loader helpers for unit and integration tests.
Baseline assertion helpers for exact affected refs and findings.

A0 includes exact affected projects/contracts/integration points/tasks/policies/evidence refs.
F0 includes at least one cross-project dependency and one stale/missing evidence condition.
Fixture load order is deterministic and reusable by later phase tests.
constraints: Do not create isolated ad hoc fixtures for each later test family.
Fixture refs remain pointer-only and stable.
tasks.comment: Completed golden fixture backbone with prohibited legacy consumer references removed. Fixture covers Hoplon, Phalanx, Semantix, Guardrail, and Program Manager MCP; A0/F0 exact refs and deterministic loader tests pass.

tasks.title: Neo4j migrations and graph repository
tasks.goal: Implement graph constraints, indexes, migrations, and ProgramManagerGraphRepository with deterministic traversal ordering and integration-test coverage.
expected_changes: Neo4j migrations for stable PMO IDs, relationship IDs, indexes, and constraints supported by the available edition.
ProgramManagerGraphRepository implementation.
Testcontainers Neo4j integration tests for persistence and traversal.
Explicit ORDER BY behavior at traversal boundaries.
definition_of_done: Repository unit and Neo4j integration tests pass.
Constraints/indexes bootstrap cleanly from an empty database.
Graph projection can persist programs, projects, memberships, contracts, relationships, decisions, evidence refs, artifact refs, and sync cursors.
Repeated traversal over the same fixture returns identical ordering and hash inputs.
constraints: Use first-class Neo4j relationships for hot traversal; avoid duplicating every edge as a node unless audit metadata requires it.
No uncontrolled dual writes without an explicit event/projection policy.

context.liveNeo4jSmoke: Added package-owned npm run smoke:neo4j runner that starts disposable neo4j:5.26-community via Docker when PMO_NEO4J_URI is not supplied, waits for Bolt connectivity, runs the live Neo4j repository test, and cleans up the container.
context.liveNeo4jCoverage: Live Neo4j test now applies migrations, seeds the golden repository fixture, verifies ordered program/project/dependency reads, and verifies deterministic impact findings through ProgramManagerGraphRepository backed by Neo4j.
context.neo4jDriverFixes: Added neo4j-driver dependency, normalized omitted/undefined Cypher parameters to null, and changed typed PmRef upserts to merge the generic PmRef node before adding typed labels so generic uniqueness constraints permit typed label enrichment.

tasks.comment: Completed Neo4j migration and graph repository layer. Added constraints/index migrations, graph store seam, repository implementation with deterministic ordering, non-live graph tests, and env-gated live Neo4j hook. Verified tests and TypeScript compile.

comments.text: Neo4j live validation upgraded: npm run smoke:neo4j starts disposable Docker Neo4j or uses PMO_NEO4J_URI, applies migrations, seeds the golden fixture, validates ordered graph reads and impact findings, and exits cleanly. Fixed live-only parameter normalization and PmRef typed-label upsert issues exposed by the smoke run.

stateVersionHash matches H0 across repeated runs.

tasks.comment: Completed seed graph and hash stability proof. Added deterministic state hash helpers, traversal budget defaults, fixture seed coverage for graph repository, and tests proving repeated H0 hash stability and deterministic traversal behavior.

Boundary tests proving no tracker, Hoplon, GitHub, Guardrail, Semantix, or Phalanx mutation happens through PMO.

Authz tests deny cross-portfolio reads and unauthorized actor scopes.

No-mutation tests prove the MCP facade only reads PMO/adapters and never calls mutation-capable downstream APIs.

Every meaningful tool result carries trace/correlation/evidence/provenance context.
tasks.comment: Completed Phase 1A MCP gateway tools, authz, redaction, and no-mutation boundary tests. All four public tools return parseable envelopes; authz/redaction/boundary tests pass; full package tests and TypeScript compile pass.

Do not make LLM Tracker the PMO source of truth.
tasks.comment: Completed read-only adapter registry with LLM Tracker and Hoplon stubs. Manifests, bounded reads, impact hooks, cursor/health/evidence behavior, and no-mutation conformance tests pass.

tasks.goal: Enhance query_program_context so agents receive bounded, evidence-backed PMO packets instead of raw database state or chat-memory instructions.
expected_changes: Context panes for currentState, blockingDependencies, applicableDecisions, supersededDecisions, futureDecisions, staleEvidence, and recommendedActions.

tasks.goal: Generate reproducible PMO alignment reports and machine-readable evidence envelopes from PMO state without making reports the source of truth.
expected_changes: Markdown report generator for alignment/update artifacts.
JSON evidence envelope containing inputs, stateVersionHash, artifact refs, evidence refs, template version, and deterministic section ordering.
Report deletion/regeneration tests.
definition_of_done: Regenerated report output is stable for the same fixture inputs and template version.
JSON evidence refs match fixture expectations.
Report artifact metadata points back to PMO state, evidence, and hash inputs.
Deleting and regenerating the report reproduces the expected artifact/evidence envelope.
constraints: Reports are generated artifacts, not canonical PMO memory.
Do not embed raw logs, screenshots, prompts, or transcript payloads in report evidence envelopes.

context.notes: Reports remain generated artifacts, not PMO source of truth; evidence envelope is pointer-only and deterministic.

tasks.comment: Completed Phase 1B report generator and JSON evidence envelope. tasks.title: Adapter health, circuit behavior, and stale cursors
tasks.goal: Add deterministic adapter health handling so degraded, stale, or unavailable sources affect confidence and findings without corrupting deterministic core state.
expected_changes: Adapter health status model: healthy, degraded, stale, unavailable.
Retry/backoff and circuit-break reset behavior for read adapters.
Stale cursor policy and degraded-mode caps.
Tests for degraded/unavailable/circuit/stale-cursor behavior.
definition_of_done: Adapter health affects warnings/findings consistently for fixture scenarios.
Stale cursors cannot satisfy current evidence freshness requirements silently.
Circuit/degraded states are visible through PMO envelopes and reports.
Deterministic core excludes unreliable model/advisory inference while preserving adapter health facts.
constraints: Do not let adapter failures trigger downstream mutation through PMO.
Keep health and freshness evidence pointer-based and traceable.

context.notes: No downstream mutations were introduced; unhealthy adapter data affects confidence/warnings without silently satisfying freshness.

tasks.comment: Completed Phase 1B adapter health, circuit behavior, and stale cursors. Added stale health status, read/impact circuit state with backoff and reset, health-derived gating/warnings/evidence refs, stale adapter skipping, degraded adapter caps, and tests for circuit recovery, stale cursors, degraded reads, and impact caps.

generate_program_update tool returning markdown artifact refs plus JSON evidence envelope refs.

Authz tests prevent cross-portfolio audit/report reads.

tasks.title: Phase 1C acceptance proof
tasks.goal: Close Phase 1C by proving evidence-backed intelligence, anchor-scoped decision behavior, deterministic matching, and advisory hash exclusion.
expected_changes: Phase 1C evidence report or implementation report.
Verification artifacts for learning queries, discarded-decision matching, anchor scoping, and model-assisted hash exclusion.
definition_of_done: Cross-program learning queries return evidence-backed issue cards.
Superseded, discarded, and future-not-applicable decisions are not applied to the wrong anchor.
Discarded decision typed-tag matching is deterministic.
Model-assisted advisory cards are labeled and excluded from deterministic hashes.
constraints: Do not proceed to flight-plan planning until intelligence semantics are stable and non-mutating.
Acceptance proof must be reproducible from fixture/test evidence.

context.notes: Verification: npm run typecheck; node --test tests/program-tools.test.js; node --test tests/golden-fixture-seed.test.js; npm test.

tasks.comment: Closed: Phase 1C acceptance proof added with typecheck, focused tool tests, fixture-seed test, and full package test evidence.

tasks.title: Deterministic flight-plan model and planner
tasks.goal: Implement plan_program_action as a deterministic PMO planner that turns proposed work into affected scope, risks, approvals, evidence obligations, and expected receipts.
expected_changes: FlightPlan schema and deterministic planner service.
plan_program_action MCP tool wiring.
flightPlanHash implementation over state hash, context anchor, proposed change, adapter manifest versions, and rule versions.
Fixture scenarios for stable repeated planning.
definition_of_done: Same inputs produce the same deterministic flight plan and flightPlanHash.
Planner returns affected projects/contracts/tasks/policies/evidence refs and explicit proposed external actions.
Advisory content is excluded from flightPlanHash.
Planner does not execute downstream actions.
constraints: A PMO flight plan is not execution authority beyond an executor's delegated scope.
Do not implement receipt recording or reconciliation here; those belong to Phase 3.
tasks.comment: Closed: plan_program_action MCP wiring, FlightPlan schemas, deterministic planner service, flightPlanHash/state hash inputs, and stable repeated-planning tests landed.

tasks.title: Approval, evidence, expected receipt, TTL, and stale-plan behavior
tasks.goal: Model the obligations a flight plan creates, including human approval gates, evidence requirements, expected receipts, expiration, and stale-plan revalidation.
expected_changes: ExpectedReceipt model and receipt requirement generation.
Approval/evidence obligation model with hard block/warn behavior by policy.
Flight plan TTL and stale-plan behavior.
Tests for unsatisfied HITL gates, missing evidence, advisory exclusion, and stale revalidation.
definition_of_done: Unsatisfied required approvals appear as hard blocks.
Missing evidence blocks or warns according to EvidencePolicy and criticality.
Expired/stale plans require revalidation before receipts can satisfy obligations.
Expected receipts carry trace, correlation, idempotency, scope, required verifier, and evidence requirements.
constraints: Do not let old plan hashes satisfy current state when stateVersionHash, rules, manifests, or anchors changed.
Approval authority must be explicit; no hidden default approval by PMO.
tasks.comment: Closed: planner now models unsatisfied approval hard blocks, evidence obligations, expected receipts, TTL expiry, and stale-plan revalidation requirements.

tasks.title: Tracker and decision proposals without downstream mutation
tasks.goal: Let flight plans propose tracker updates and decision requests while preserving the PMO boundary that execution agents perform actual writes.
expected_changes: Proposal payloads for tracker comments/blockers/tasks, report updates, verification reruns, and decision requests.
No-mutation enforcement tests for tracker/GitHub/Hoplon/Semantix/Guardrail/Phalanx operations.
Receipt expectations tied to proposed external actions.
definition_of_done: PMO output clearly separates proposed external actions from actions already performed.
Tracker update proposals identify target slug/task/ref, rationale, approval/evidence needs, and expected receipt shape.
Decision request proposals include authority required, scope, due/review behavior, affected refs, and evidence obligations.
Tests prove PMO does not call tracker write endpoints or other mutation-capable downstream APIs.
constraints: Execution agents own downstream mutations and must return receipts.
Avoid hiding external side effects behind adapter method names.
tasks.comment: Closed: tracker/decision/external work is represented as proposal-only actions with receipt expectations; boundary tests prove PMO does not execute downstream mutation APIs.

tasks.title: Circular propagation suppression and repeated-plan stability
tasks.goal: Prevent adapter-to-adapter feedback loops and prove repeated planning is stable under unchanged deterministic inputs.
expected_changes: Propagation ancestry, causation, idempotency, and suppression metadata.
Loop-detection tests for repeated proposed actions and adapter feedback.
Repeated-plan stability tests over unchanged state hash, context anchor, proposed change, manifests, and rules.
definition_of_done: A proposed propagation action carries enough ancestry/causation metadata to avoid recursive loops.
Repeated flight plan generation under identical deterministic inputs is stable.
Suppressed duplicate/looping proposals are visible as findings or warnings with evidence refs.
Suppression does not hide genuinely new state changes with new hashes/cursors.
constraints: Propagation must be loop-safe and idempotent.
Cross-tool execution is not a PMO transaction; partial completion remains explicit.
tasks.comment: Closed: propagation ancestry, idempotency keys, repeated-edge suppression, and deterministic repeated-plan stability are implemented and tested.

tasks.title: Phase 2 acceptance proof
tasks.goal: Close Phase 2 by proving deterministic flight plans, approval gates, evidence obligations, proposal-only downstream actions, and stale-plan behavior.
expected_changes: Phase 2 evidence report or implementation report.
Verification artifacts for stable flightPlanHash, no tracker writes, HITL gates, evidence policy behavior, advisory exclusion, and stale-plan revalidation.
definition_of_done: No tracker writes occur through PMO.
Repeated flight plans are stable under the same deterministic inputs.
Unsatisfied HITL approval gates appear as hard blocks.
Missing evidence blocks or warns according to policy.
Expired or stale plans require revalidation before receipts can satisfy them.
constraints: Do not move to receipt ledger until expected receipt obligations and no-mutation behavior are proven.
Acceptance proof must include reproducible test or fixture evidence.
tasks.comment: Closed: Phase 2 acceptance proof documents deterministic flight plans, no tracker writes, approval/evidence behavior, advisory exclusion, receipt expectations, and stale-plan revalidation evidence.

tasks.title: Expected-vs-observed receipt ledger and audit events
tasks.goal: Implement PMO-owned ledger storage for expected receipts, observed receipts, action ledger entries, audit events, and reconcile status.
expected_changes: Ledger schemas/repository support for ActionLedgerEntry, ExpectedReceipt, ObservedReceipt, audit events, and reconcile status.
Persistence model linking receipts to flight plans, evidence refs, actors, trace/correlation, idempotency keys, and affected scope.
Fixture scenarios for accepted, missing, late, lost, duplicate, and conflicting receipts.
definition_of_done: Expected and observed receipts can be persisted and queried by plan, action, project, contract, actor, and evidence refs.
Audit events are append-only and replay-compatible.
Ledger state represents partial completion explicitly.
Ledger records do not imply downstream mutation was performed unless an observed receipt/evidence supports it.
constraints: PMO records receipts and reconciles state; it still does not execute downstream actions.
Every ledger entry must preserve trace, correlation, actor, scope, and evidence context.
tasks.comment: Completed Phase 3 receipt ledger storage foundation: schemas/domain types, repository interface, in-memory/graph/Neo4j persistence/query methods, append-only audit events, and focused tests for schema validation, filtering, partial reconcile state, and audit ordering. Verified typecheck, schema generation, focused ledger test, and full npm test (49 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: record_program_receipt tool and validation
tasks.goal: Expose receipt submission with validation for digest, signature, operator attestation, authz, idempotency, stale plan state, and required evidence.
expected_changes: record_program_receipt MCP tool contract and gateway wiring.
Receipt validation service for expected receipt matching, digest/signature/operator-attestation methods, actor authority, idempotency, and stale-plan checks.
Tests for accepted, rejected, duplicate, incomplete, forged, and stale flight-plan receipts.
definition_of_done: Valid receipts are recorded exactly once and linked to expected receipt obligations.
Unauthorized, forged, incomplete, duplicate, or stale-plan receipts are rejected with standard envelope warnings/errors.
Receipt validation respects EvidencePolicy and approval authority rules.
Accepted receipts update PMO ledger/projections without mutating downstream systems.
constraints: Do not trust receipt claims without digest/signature/operator-attestation or adapter-observed evidence as required by policy.
Idempotency must prevent repeated receipt submission from corrupting ledger state.
tasks.comment: Completed record_program_receipt. Added request/core/result schemas, gateway/tool listing, Phase 3 capability listing, validation for expected match, digest, evidence, verifier, idempotency duplicate, stale plan hash, operator authz path, PMO-only ledger writes, and tests for accepted, duplicate, forged, incomplete, stale, and unauthorized receipts. Verified typecheck, schema generation, and full npm test (51 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: reconcile_program_state tool and findings
tasks.goal: Compare expected receipts and PMO graph state against observed adapter state to raise missing, stale, conflicting, or desynchronized findings.
expected_changes: reconcile_program_state MCP tool contract and gateway wiring.
Reconciliation service comparing expected receipts, observed receipts, adapter state, dependency edges, evidence freshness, and sync cursors.
Desynchronization finding types and compensating/replacement flight plan proposal payloads.
definition_of_done: Reconciliation detects one accepted receipt with another missing in fixture scenarios.
Conflicting adapter-observed state versus receipt claims produces a deterministic finding.
Late/lost/stuck due-policy timing is surfaced with evidence refs and recommended next action.
Compensating/replacement plan proposals are generated without executing downstream mutation.
constraints: Adapter observations can degrade confidence but must be represented with health/freshness metadata.
Do not hide partial failures behind an overall success status.
tasks.comment: Completed reconcile_program_state. Added request/core/result schemas, gateway/tool/capability wiring, ledger-vs-adapter reconciliation, satisfied/in_flight/late/lost/conflicting statuses, deterministic findings, replacement proposal payloads, PMO reconcile status persistence, and tests for accepted+lost partial state and conflicting receipt claims. Verified typecheck, schema generation, and full npm test (53 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: Receipt security, idempotency, due-policy, and conflict tests
tasks.goal: Harden receipt and reconciliation behavior with tests for authz rejection, duplicates, stale plan receipts, due-policy timing, forgery, incompleteness, and conflicting observed state.
expected_changes: Receipt validation test suite.
Reconciliation conflict/staleness test suite.
Due-policy timing tests for in_flight, late, lost, and stuck plans.
Fixture additions for forged, incomplete, duplicate, stale, missing, and conflicting receipts.
definition_of_done: Authz rejection tests pass.
Duplicate receipt and idempotency tests pass.
Stale flight-plan receipt tests pass.
Due-policy timing tests pass for in_flight, late, lost, and stuck states.
Forged/incomplete/conflicting-state tests produce deterministic findings or rejections.
constraints: Tests must not require live downstream mutation.
Security failures must return traceable evidence and not merely generic errors.
tasks.comment: Completed Phase 3 receipt hardening coverage. Added explicit stuck reconcile status and due-policy logic; tests now cover authz rejection, duplicate/idempotency, stale plan receipts, forged digest, incomplete evidence, accepted+missing partial reconcile, in_flight/late/lost/stuck timing, and conflicting observed state. Verified typecheck, schema generation, and full npm test (54 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: Phase 3 acceptance proof
tasks.goal: Close Phase 3 by proving receipt ledger, validation, idempotency, due policy, reconciliation, and desynchronization behavior.
expected_changes: Phase 3 evidence report or implementation report.

Verification artifacts for receipt validation, authz rejection, idempotency, stale receipts, due-policy timing, forged/incomplete receipt rejection, conflicting state, and mixed accepted/missing reconciliation.
definition_of_done: Receipt validation tests pass.
Authz rejection, duplicate receipt, idempotency, stale flight-plan, due-policy, forged/incomplete, and conflicting-state tests pass.
Reconciliation tests show one receipt accepted and another missing.
Desynchronization findings include evidence refs and proposed next actions.
constraints: Do not proceed to Agentic OS integration until receipt semantics and reconciliation are stable.
Acceptance evidence must be reproducible through test commands or artifact refs.
tasks.comment: Completed Phase 3 acceptance proof. Evidence covers receipt validation, authz, idempotency, stale plans, due-policy reconciliation, missing/accepted receipts, conflict findings, and proposal-only recovery. Verification: npm test passes 55 tests, 54 pass, 1 skipped.

tasks.title: Agentic OS context packet and receipt submission integration
tasks.goal: Wire Agentic OS workflows to consume PMO context packets and submit receipts back into Program Manager after execution agents perform external work.
expected_changes: Agentic OS integration points for PMO context packet retrieval.
Receipt submission path from execution agents to record_program_receipt.
End-to-end fixture flow from proposed work through PMO context, executor action outside PMO, receipt submission, and ledger update.
definition_of_done: Agentic OS can request PMO context packets and graph refs for a work context.
Execution agents can submit receipts back to PMO with trace/correlation/evidence context.
Integration preserves the passive analyst boundary: PMO does not perform downstream execution.
Context packet and receipt contracts stay compatible with Phase 1B/3 tests.
constraints: Do not make PMO the hidden executor in Agentic OS workflows.
Keep external side effects in specialized execution agents and require receipts back to PMO.
tasks.comment: Completed Agentic OS context packet and receipt submission integration. Added get_agentic_os_context_packet and submit_agentic_os_receipt as wrappers over PMO context, proposal-only flight plan, and receipt ledger tools. Carries cp-graph refs, governance refs, passive-boundary metadata, and receipt handoff. Verified typecheck, npm test, doctor, replay/report, otel, and phase4 smoke.

tasks.title: Trust root, tenant isolation, retention, PII, and redaction hardening
tasks.goal: Harden production-facing security and governance controls for verified identity, portfolio isolation, trust roots, retention policy, PII handling, and redaction enforcement.
expected_changes: Trust-root integration for server-verified actor identity.
Tenant/portfolio isolation hardening for all public tools and operational paths.
Retention and PII policies for PMO events, artifact refs, evidence refs, actor metadata, reports, and receipts.
Expanded redaction tests and operational policy checks.
definition_of_done: Tenant isolation tests pass across read, plan, receipt, reconcile, audit, and report paths.
Auth trust-root tests verify actor identity and scoped authority.
Retention/redaction tests prove sensitive or expired data is omitted, redacted, or blocked according to policy.
Tier 0/1 approval and evidence behavior remains enforceable after hardening.
constraints: Do not store secrets, credentials, raw sessions, or content-bearing evidence inline.
RetentionPolicyRef must resolve to concrete policy before production-style operations.
tasks.comment: Completed trust root, isolation, retention, PII, and redaction hardening. Agentic OS schemas require governance refs; envelopes carry trust-root/retention/PII policy refs; execution-agent receipt submission blocks authenticated actor mismatches; existing authz still enforces cross-portfolio/project scope. Verified authz tests, full npm test, typecheck, doctor, and phase4 smoke.

tasks.title: OpenTelemetry and operational replay/report checks
tasks.goal: Add production observability and operational checks for PMO metrics, traces, logs, replay, report regeneration, and degraded adapter behavior.
expected_changes: OpenTelemetry metrics, traces, and logs for PMO tool calls, adapter reads, plan generation, receipt validation, reconciliation, replay, and report generation.
Operational replay and report regeneration checks integrated into runbooks or CI/ops smoke.
Adapter degraded/circuit evidence surfaced in observability.
definition_of_done: OTel smoke tests pass and show trace/correlation propagation through PMO flows.
Replay and report regeneration are part of operational checks.
Adapter degraded/circuit states emit useful telemetry and evidence refs.
Observability output does not leak raw sensitive/content-bearing payloads.
constraints: Telemetry must respect redaction and pointer-only evidence rules.
Operational checks should distinguish unavailable/degraded dependencies from PMO logic failures.
tasks.comment: Completed OTel and operational replay/report checks. Added redaction-safe telemetry helpers, pmo:otel-smoke, focused telemetry tests, and replay/report/degraded adapter telemetry evidence. Verified pmo:otel-smoke, full npm test, typecheck, doctor, replay smoke, and report regeneration.

tasks.goal: Prove the hardened Program Manager path works end to end across context packets, execution-agent receipts, reconciliation, tenant isolation, degraded adapters, replay, retention, and OTel evidence.
expected_changes: End-to-end smoke scenario and evidence artifact.
Verification evidence for context packets, receipts, reconciliation, tenant isolation, trust root, degraded adapters, schema migration/replay, retention/redaction, and OTel.
definition_of_done: End-to-end workflow smoke passes.
Context packet, tenant isolation, auth trust-root, adapter degraded/circuit, schema migration/replay, retention/redaction, and OTel smoke tests pass.
Smoke evidence includes artifact/evidence refs and trace/correlation IDs.
Any unresolved production hardening gaps are recorded as explicit follow-up tasks or decision gates.
constraints: Do not call external production mutation paths without explicit operator approval.
Smoke must preserve PMO as analyst/planner/ledger, not executor.
tasks.comment: Completed end-to-end Agentic OS operational smoke. Added pmo:phase4-smoke covering context packet creation, expected receipt seeding for simulated external execution, submit_agentic_os_receipt, reconciliation satisfaction, and telemetry evidence. Phase 4 verification is documented in docs/phase-4/acceptance-proof.md.

tasks.title: Macro object model and envelope contract alignment
tasks.goal: Normalize the pmo_macro object model and deterministic envelope contracts against the implementation prompt and the existing PMO schemas before adding runtime behavior.
expected_changes: Zod schemas and generated JSON Schema coverage for Portfolio, Program, Project, Task, Blocker, Contract, DependencyEdge, Runbook, FlightPlan, ExpectedReceipt, ObservedReceipt, Finding, RiskSignal, EvidenceRef, ArtifactRef, macro registry entries, macro invocation requests, and result envelopes.
Canonical hash input rules for pmo_macro deterministic core, stateVersionHash, evidence refs, artifact refs, redaction summaries, trace ids, correlation ids, and advisoryPane exclusion.
Fixture examples for valid and invalid normalized objects with explicit portfolio/program/project/repo/branch/tracker/time/source/evidence scoping.
definition_of_done: All persisted PMO objects that can affect planning require portfolio scope and scoped fact fields where applicable.
Schema tests prove pointer-only evidence/artifact refs and reject raw logs, secrets, full diffs, transcripts, screenshots, and provider payloads.
Hash input fixtures are deterministic and exclude advisoryPane/model-assisted text.
constraints: Preserve PMO as a stateful analyst, planner, ledger, and reconciler, not a downstream executor.
Do not break existing Phase 0 through Phase 4 public contracts.

context.execution_assumption: Existing Program Manager MCP roadmap through pmo-604 is complete before this lane starts.
context.verification: npm run typecheck; npm test; focused schema/hash tests; Hoplon describe_project and relevant-test oracle (conservative).
context.notes: Runtime pmo_macro dispatcher intentionally left for later Phase 5 tasks.

tasks.comment: Completed Phase 5 macro object model and envelope contract alignment. Added pmo_macro object/registry/request/core/result/hash schemas, Phase 5 valid/invalid/hash/tool fixtures, generated JSON Schema snapshots, and hash canonical exclusions for traceId/correlationId. Verification: typecheck and full package test pass (63 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: Macro fixture universe and golden scenarios
tasks.goal: Build deterministic seed fixtures and expected outputs for blocker alleviation, catch-me-up context, impact simulation, and drift/reality-check macros.
expected_changes: Seed graph fixture data for the normalized object model across at least Hoplon, Phalanx, Semantix, Guardrail, and Program Manager MCP.
Golden macro request and response fixtures for blocker analysis, localized context, cross-domain simulation, and drift detection.
Expected stateVersionHash, evidenceRefs, artifactRefs, warnings, redaction summaries, and degraded adapter examples.
definition_of_done: Fixtures are parseable through the new schemas and load into the repository test harness deterministically.
Golden outputs use stable ordering and exact assertions for deterministic response cores.
Fixture evidence remains pointer-only and unevidenced/advisory records are explicitly marked.
constraints: Do not use LLM Tracker as PMO durable memory; tracker refs are source pointers only.
Do not inline long reports or logs in fixtures.

context.verification: npm run typecheck; npm test; focused schema fixture test; Hoplon relevant-test oracle (conservative).
context.notes: Golden scenarios are pointer-only and include explicit stateVersionHash/evidence/artifact/warning/redaction expectations.

tasks.comment: Completed Phase 5 macro fixture universe and golden scenarios. Added schema-backed seed graph covering Hoplon, Phalanx, Semantix, Guardrail, and Program Manager MCP plus exact pmo_macro golden scenarios for analyze_blockers, catch_me_up, detect_drift, and simulate_impact. Verification: typecheck and full package test pass (64 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: Portfolio-scoped repository and Neo4j macro support
tasks.goal: Extend the graph repository and migrations so pmo_macro reads and PMO-owned configuration mutations are portfolio-safe, ordered, and deterministic.
expected_changes: Neo4j labels, relationship types, constraints, and indexes for normalized macro objects and macro registry records.
Repository methods for bounded traversal, current fact selection, dependency impact reads, blocker/runbook lookup, drift comparison inputs, and macro registry persistence.
Migration idempotency and stable-order query tests for every deterministic macro input path.
definition_of_done: Repository calls require portfolio scope or explicit cross-portfolio authority and deny cross-portfolio reads by default.
Macro logic consumes repository abstractions rather than embedding raw Cypher in dispatcher/runtime code.
Repeated reads over the same fixture produce the same ordered deterministic core.
constraints: PMO-owned writes are limited to PMO repository, projections, event log, artifact registry, macro registry, findings, reports, receipts, and reconciliation state.
Simulation reads may not persist hypothetical program truth.

context.risk: Nondeterministic read ordering would invalidate stateVersionHash assertions.
context.completion_summary: Extended PMO macro repository and graph-store support for portfolio-scoped deterministic macro facts and macro registry persistence. Added domain/interface types, in-memory and graph-backed read/write methods, Neo4j storage hooks, V003 macro object migration, and deterministic fixture tests covering ordering, target filters, and cross-portfolio isolation.
context.verification: npm run typecheck; node --test tests/program-manager-graph-repository.test.js tests/program-manager-graph-neo4j.test.js tests/in-memory-program-manager-repository.test.js; npm test (67 pass, 1 skipped). Hoplon get_relevant_tests returned conservative/no direct test mapping, so full package suite was run.

tasks.comment: Starting pmo-703. Extending repository and graph-store macro fixture support with portfolio-scoped deterministic reads and PMO-owned macro registry persistence.

comments.author: codex

tasks.title: Editable safe macro registry
tasks.goal: Implement the built-in macro registry and authorized edit path while preventing edits from granting downstream execution authority or unsafe side-effect classes.
expected_changes: Built-in registry entries for help, discovery, documentation, validation, context, impact simulation, blocker analysis, unblock plan proposals, drift detection, registry export, and object-type docs.
Role/scope checks for registry edits plus audit events for accepted and rejected edits.
Locked-field validation for sideEffectClass, downstream authority, deterministic hash policy, required evidence behavior, and portfolio isolation rules.
definition_of_done: Authorized edits can change safe descriptive/configuration fields and are stored only as PMO internal configuration mutations.
Unauthorized or unsafe edits are rejected with deterministic errors and audit evidence refs.
Tests prove registry edits cannot expose direct downstream mutations or hidden execution authority.
constraints: Macro editing must not grant GitHub, LLM Tracker, Hoplon, Semantix, Guardrail, Phalanx, Serena, or other downstream mutation authority.
Client-supplied actor ids are not authoritative for edit authorization.

context.security_boundary: Internal PMO config mutation only; no downstream mutation authority.
context.completion_summary: Implemented an editable safe PMO macro registry module with built-in sorted macro entries, safe edit whitelist, locked-field validation for side-effect posture/schema/authority/hash/evidence policy fields, server-verified actor role and portfolio checks, and a repository-backed persist path that stores accepted edits only as PMO internal macro registry mutations with audit events.
context.verification: npm run generate:schemas; npm run typecheck; node --test tests/pmo-macro-registry.test.js tests/program-manager-graph-repository.test.js tests/program-manager-schemas.test.js; npm test (71 pass, 1 skipped). Hoplon get_relevant_tests returned conservative/no direct test mapping, so full package suite was run.

tasks.comment: Starting pmo-704. Implementing the built-in macro registry and authorized edit path while preserving the PMO non-executor boundary.

comments.author: codex
comments.body: Completed pmo-704. Added built-in safe macro registry and repository-backed edit path. Accepted edits are limited to descriptive/config fields and stored as PMO registry mutations with audit events; unauthorized, cross-portfolio, locked-field, and downstream-authority edits are deterministic rejections. Verification: generate:schemas, typecheck, focused macro/schema/repository tests, npm test (71 pass, 1 skipped).

tasks.title: Single public pmo_macro dispatcher
tasks.goal: Expose exactly one public PMO MCP tool named pmo_macro with help, discovery, validation, invocation, object-type documentation, export, and authorized registry editing.
expected_changes: MCP dispatcher for pmo_macro actions such as help, list, describe, validate, invoke, edit_registry, export_registry, list_object_types, and describe_object_type.
Standard result envelope wiring for trace ids, correlation ids, evidence refs, artifact refs, warnings, redaction summaries, state hashes, and advisory panes.
Public tool inventory tests proving PMO does not expose dozens of separate MCP tools.

Every invocation is schema-validated before execution and returns a deterministic envelope or deterministic validation error.

constraints: Do not turn pmo_macro into an arbitrary downstream tool proxy.
Pure help/documentation operations may omit stateVersionHash; planning, impact, simulation, intelligence, or reconciliation results may not.

tasks.comment: Starting pmo-705. Adding the single public pmo_macro dispatcher over the safe registry and repository-backed macro inputs.

tasks.comment: Starting pmo-706. Implementing catch_me_up and simulate_impact invocations behind pmo_macro using bounded repository reads and no truth mutation.

tasks.title: Blocker analysis and proposed unblock plans
tasks.goal: Implement blocker alleviation macros that classify blockers, map them to runbooks, and propose external work through ProposedAction, FlightPlan, and ExpectedReceipt records.
expected_changes: Macro implementations for blocker analysis and proposed unblock plans over normalized blockers, tasks, contracts, dependencies, runbooks, decisions, and evidence refs.
ProposedAction, FlightPlan, and ExpectedReceipt outputs that name the external executor authority without PMO performing the work.
Tests for blocker typing, runbook linkage, approval/evidence obligations, expected receipt generation, and no downstream mutation calls.
definition_of_done: At least four representative blocker scenarios produce deterministic typed recommendations and expected receipts.
Outputs distinguish proposed external actions from actions already performed and never claim PMO executed downstream work.
No-mutation boundary tests cover LLM Tracker, Hoplon, GitHub, Guardrail, Semantix, Phalanx, Serena, and adapter stubs.
constraints: PMO may plan, propose, ledger, reconcile, and report; execution agents perform external mutations using their own tools.
Approval authority must be explicit and evidence-referenced.

Normalize evidenceRefs/artifactRefs sorting and URI validation before writes where deterministic ordering matters.

tasks.title: Add autonomous three-agent integration-loop acceptance proof
tasks.goal: Prove the exact user-requested flow works end to end with independent agents using MCP tools only.
expected_changes: Create an integration-test fixture or scripted MCP stdio scenario with three independent agent personas.

Use fixed fixture identifiers: portfolio://pmo-omni-fixture, program:pmo-omni-shared-flow, project:pmo-producer, project:pmo-consumer-a, project:pmo-consumer-b, integration://pmo-omni/shared-flow, agent:pmo-producer, agent:pmo-consumer-a, agent:pmo-consumer-b, and lt://program-manager-mcp/pmo-omni-fixture-task refs.
Agent 1 reads help, registers an integration through manage_integrations, publishes goals/tasks, and adds participant projects.
Agents 2 and 3 read the integration through manage_integrations and submit gaps, issues, blockers, project responses, artifacts, linked LT task refs, and plan notes.
Agent 1 reads the consolidated state, sees actionable gaps/blockers/conflicts, and records follow-up actions or decision requests.

The scenario exercises default authority policy: reporter/blocked project controls unblock/resolve, owner project submits resolution evidence, affected project can acknowledge/dispute, unrelated project is rejected, and PMO does not infer closure.
The scenario exercises blocker lifecycle: report, owner response with evidence, blocked-project unblock/resolve, and reopen.
The scenario exercises wrong-agent protection by attempting an unauthorized unblock/resolve and asserting useful guidance.
The scenario exercises project inbox/catch_up outputs for each participant.
PMO continues syncing through durable records and relay panes without requiring humans to drive context.
definition_of_done: The proof runs as an automated test or deterministic smoke script.
The scenario asserts PMO did not mutate project trackers or fabricate decisions.
The scenario verifies that new agent context can be recovered after clearing in-memory conversation state.
The scenario uses the fixed fixture IDs and does not require human-provided setup values.
The scenario verifies conflicts are surfaced and not auto-resolved.
The scenario verifies idempotent retries do not duplicate blockers, responses, decisions, artifacts, or learnings.
The scenario verifies stale updates and wrong-agent updates are rejected with clear next actions.
constraints: Use MCP tool calls, not private in-process helpers, for the acceptance path.
Keep the fixture small but representative.
The proof must start from pmo_help so agent bootstrap quality is tested.
The proof must use manage_integrations as the canonical integration tool.

tasks.title: Update help and docs for agent-only PMO bootstrap
tasks.goal: Make agent-facing help self-contained so agents can start from the help tool, discover domain omni-tools, recover from missing data, and coordinate without external docs.
expected_changes: Update pmo_help to explain when to use manage_program_items, manage_integrations, manage_evidence_items, manage_decision_items, manage_learning_items, manage_adapter_items, and pmo_macro.
Add examples for the first agent, participant project agents, and returning agents after memory loss.

Ensure help identifies manage_integrations as the canonical integration omni-tool and does not route agents to manage_integration_items.
Update README and agent onboarding docs after tool behavior is implemented.
definition_of_done: An agent can read pmo_help and perform the three-agent integration loop without repo docs.
Missing identifiers and blocked validation responses include concrete next tool/action suggestions.
Docs and help agree with tools/list schemas and tests.
constraints: Docs are secondary; MCP help must carry the operational instructions.
Keep pmo_macro documented only as macro automation on top of PMO data and tools.

tasks.title: Cypher rule detector architecture
tasks.goal: Track the implementation of versioned, editable Cypher detector definitions that run against Neo4j, write controlled Findings, and feed reports and health dashboards. PMO skill concepts belong in the rule catalog as groups, rule metadata, report definitions, parameters, and editable seeds, not as new canonical PMO source-data entities unless a later task explicitly adds the source-data contract.

context.notes: Catalog-first clarification added after data check: use editable Rule/RuleVersion/RuleGroup/ReportDefinition records for PMO skill rule packs; do not introduce Schedule/Cost/etc. or resource/risk planning concepts as first-class PMO graph facts just to make detectors possible.

tasks.title: Define rule graph schema and indexes
tasks.goal: Add the graph model, relationships, uniqueness constraints, and lookup indexes for Rule, RuleVersion, RuleGroup, RuleRun, Finding, ReportDefinition, and HealthSnapshot.
definition_of_done: Graph objects and relationships are represented in the project schema or migration layer for the rule engine core only: Rule, RuleVersion, RuleGroup, RuleRun, Finding, ReportDefinition, and HealthSnapshot.
Uniqueness constraints exist for rule keys, rule version IDs, rule group keys, finding fingerprints, and project IDs.
Indexes cover finding status, finding rule/status, project health, and rule enabled state.

PMO framework labels such as Schedule, Cost, Scope, Quality, Risk, Stakeholder, Resource Allocation, and Portfolio Planning are modeled as editable catalog metadata or RuleGroups, not as new canonical source-data node types.
expected_changes: Neo4j schema/migrations
Schema fixtures or setup docs
constraints: Do not add HealthDimension, Resource, Risk, or portfolio-planning domain nodes only to support a detector seed; add source-data contracts separately when actual upstream data exists.

tasks.title: Implement Rule and RuleVersion lifecycle
tasks.goal: Implement create, validate, activate, update-as-new-version, disable, retire, and draft-only hard delete semantics without overwriting active rule logic.
definition_of_done: Rule is the stable business concept and RuleVersion is the immutable logic record.
Updating a rule creates a new RuleVersion and supersedes the previous active version.
Historical Findings remain linked to the RuleVersion that generated them.
expected_changes: Rule catalog service
Rule lifecycle persistence

tasks.title: Validate read-only detector Cypher
tasks.goal: Build validation that accepts detector queries only when they are read-only, parameterized, return the standard Finding row shape, and pass parse/performance/fixture checks.
definition_of_done: Validation rejects write clauses and unsafe procedure calls in detector rules.
Validation proves required columns are returned: ruleKey, projectId, entityId, entityType, severity, weight, title, reason, recommendation, and fingerprint.
Validation can use Neo4j EXPLAIN and fixture data before activation.
constraints: Do not execute unvalidated detector Cypher with write privileges.
Use parameters rather than string-building.
expected_changes: Rule validation service
Cypher safety checks
Detector fixture tests

tasks.title: Compute health snapshots from open Findings
tasks.goal: Calculate explainable project health scores from open Finding weights and editable rule-catalog health metadata, then persist HealthSnapshot records with reason codes and status bands.
definition_of_done: Health score is derived from open Finding penalty weights and clamped to the supported range.
GREEN, AMBER, and RED status bands are applied consistently.
Schedule, Cost, Scope, Quality, Risk, and Stakeholder weights are configurable catalog metadata using the PMO defaults: 0.25, 0.20, 0.15, 0.20, 0.10, and 0.10.

Unsupported metric-specific health rules, such as SPI/CPI/EAC/defect-rate/stakeholder-satisfaction, are not enabled until source fields are present in fixtures and schema.

HealthSnapshot persistence
Dashboard/report data contract

tasks.title: Enforce detector security guardrails
tasks.goal: Harden detector authoring and execution with least-privilege Neo4j credentials, forbidden write/procedure clauses, and parameter-only execution.
definition_of_done: Detector rules cannot contain CREATE, MERGE, SET, DELETE, DETACH DELETE, REMOVE, LOAD CSV, dbms procedures, or APOC procedures unless explicitly allowed later.
Runtime detector execution uses read-only database privileges where possible.
Controlled application code remains the only path that writes Findings.
approval_required_for: Allowing arbitrary procedure calls from authored detector rules
Running detector rules with write-capable Neo4j credentials
expected_changes: Cypher allow/deny list
Neo4j credential policy

tasks.title: Complete Finding lifecycle and causal links
